server.c(3) Library Functions Manual server.c(3)

NAME

server.c - WebSocket server implementation.

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct websocket_connection_data_t
Per-connection user data.

Functions


asciichat_error_t websocket_server_init (websocket_server_t *server, const websocket_server_config_t *config)
asciichat_error_t websocket_server_run (websocket_server_t *server)
void websocket_server_cancel_service (websocket_server_t *server)
void websocket_server_destroy (websocket_server_t *server)

Detailed Description

WebSocket server implementation.

void websocket_server_cancel_service (websocket_server_t * server)

void
  websocket_server_destroy (websocket_server_t * server)


Definition at line 836 of file
    lib/network/websocket/server.c.


836                                                           {
837   if (!server) {
838     return;
839   }
840 
841   atomic_store_bool(&server->running, false);
842 
843   // Cancel any pending libwebsockets service calls to interrupt blocking lws_service()
844   // This prevents the event loop from getting stuck in lws_service()
845   if (server->context) {
846     log_debug("[WEBSOCKET_SERVER_DESTROY] Cancelling libwebsockets service");
847     lws_cancel_service(server->context);
848   }
849 
850   // Unregister atomic fields
851   NAMED_UNREGISTER(&server->running);
852 
853   // Destroy handler thread pool (waits for pending work to complete)
854   if (server->handler_pool) {
855     thread_pool_destroy(server->handler_pool);
856     server->handler_pool = NULL;
857   }
858 
859   // Context is normally destroyed by websocket_server_run (from the event loop
860   // thread) for fast shutdown. This handles the case where run() wasn't called
861   // or didn't complete normally.
862   if (server->context) {
863     NAMED_UNREGISTER(server->context);
864     log_debug("WebSocket context still alive in destroy, cleaning up");
865     lws_context_destroy(server->context);
866     server->context = NULL;
867   }
868 
869   log_debug("WebSocket server destroyed");
870 }






References atomic_store_bool(), and
    thread_pool_destroy().


Referenced by acds_main(), and server_main().






asciichat_error_t
  websocket_server_init (websocket_server_t * server, const
  websocket_server_config_t * config)


Definition at line 678 of file
    lib/network/websocket/server.c.


678                                                                                                              {
679   if (!server || !config || !config->client_handler) {
680     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters");
681   }
682 
683   memset(server, 0, sizeof(*server));
684   server->handler = config->client_handler;
685   server->user_data = config->user_data;
686   server->port = config->port;
687   atomic_store_bool(&server->running, true);
688 
689   // Store server pointer in protocol user data so callbacks can access it
690   // Both the HTTP and ACIP protocols need access to the server
691   websocket_protocols[0].user = server; // http protocol
692   websocket_protocols[1].user = server; // acip protocol
693 
694   // Enable libwebsockets logging through centralized logging system
695   lws_log_init_server();
696 
697   // Configure libwebsockets context
698   struct lws_context_creation_info info = {0};
699   info.port = config->port;
700   info.protocols = websocket_protocols;
701   info.gid = (gid_t)-1;                                // Cast to avoid undefined behavior with unsigned type
702   info.uid = (uid_t)-1;                                // Cast to avoid undefined behavior with unsigned type
703   info.options = LWS_SERVER_OPTION_DO_SSL_GLOBAL_INIT; // Initialize SSL/TLS support (required for server binding)
704   info.extensions = NULL;                              // Disable permessage-deflate - causing connection issues
705   info.retry_and_idle_policy = &keep_alive_policy; // Configure keep-alive to prevent idle disconnects during handshake
706 
707   // Configure TLS/WSS support if certificates are provided (check for non-empty strings)
708   if (config->tls_cert_path && config->tls_cert_path[0] != '\0' && config->tls_key_path &&
709       config->tls_key_path[0] != '\0') {
710 #ifdef LWS_WITH_TLS
711     // Validate certificate and key files are readable
712     if (access(config->tls_cert_path, R_OK) != 0) {
713       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_BIND, "TLS certificate file not readable: %s (errno=%d)", config->tls_cert_path, errno);
714     }
715     if (access(config->tls_key_path, R_OK) != 0) {
716       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_BIND, "TLS key file not readable: %s (errno=%d)", config->tls_key_path, errno);
717     }
718 
719     info.ssl_cert_filepath = config->tls_cert_path;
720     info.ssl_private_key_filepath = config->tls_key_path;
721     log_info("WebSocket server configured for WSS (TLS): cert=%s, key=%s", config->tls_cert_path, config->tls_key_path);
722 #else
723     log_warn("WebSocket server: TLS support not compiled in libwebsockets; WSS unavailable");
724 #endif
725   } else if ((config->tls_cert_path && config->tls_cert_path[0] != '\0') ||
726              (config->tls_key_path && config->tls_key_path[0] != '\0')) {
727     log_warn("WebSocket server: Both TLS certificate and key must be provided for WSS; using plain WS");
728   }
729 
730   // Increase per-thread service buffer to prevent fragmentation of large messages
731   // Default is 4KB, causing large frames to fragment into many chunks
732   // Video frames are 921KB, so increase to 2MB to handle them without fragmentation
733   info.pt_serv_buf_size = 2 * 1024 * 1024; // 2MB per-thread service buffer
734 
735   // Disable ALL default timeouts and keep-alive mechanisms
736   // Use only the explicit retry_and_idle_policy we set above (30/35 seconds)
737   info.ka_time = 0;           // Disable TCP keep-alive probes (use WebSocket pings instead)
738   info.ka_probes = 0;         // Disable TCP probes
739   info.ka_interval = 0;       // Disable TCP probe intervals
740   info.keepalive_timeout = 0; // Disable HTTP keep-alive timeout entirely
741 
742   // Explicitly set a very large idle timeout to prevent early disconnection
743   // Without this, libwebsockets defaults to 5 seconds for HTTP connections
744   // Set to effectively infinite (100 hours) since we use retry_and_idle_policy instead
745   info.timeout_secs = 360000; // 100 hours - effectively disabled
746 
747   // Create libwebsockets context
748   // Log diagnostic info before attempting creation
749   log_debug("lws_create_context: port=%u, protocols=%p, options=0x%x, ssl_cert=%s, ssl_key=%s",
750             info.port, (void*)info.protocols, info.options,
751             info.ssl_cert_filepath ? info.ssl_cert_filepath : "(none)",
752             info.ssl_private_key_filepath ? info.ssl_private_key_filepath : "(none)");
753 
754   server->context = lws_create_context(&info);
755   if (!server->context) {
756     // Capture any OpenSSL errors that may have occurred during context creation
757     unsigned long ssl_err = 0;
758     char ssl_err_str[256] = "no OpenSSL errors in queue";
759 
760     // Extract all errors from OpenSSL error queue
761     while ((ssl_err = ERR_get_error()) != 0) {
762       ERR_error_string_n(ssl_err, ssl_err_str, sizeof(ssl_err_str));
763       log_error("OpenSSL error: %lu: %s", ssl_err, ssl_err_str);
764     }
765 
766     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_BIND, "Failed to create libwebsockets context (last OpenSSL error: %s)", ssl_err_str);
767   }
768 
769   // Create thread pool for handling client connections
770   // Use 4 worker threads to handle concurrent client handlers without blocking LWS event loop
771   server->handler_pool = thread_pool_create_with_workers("websocket_handlers", 4);
772   if (!server->handler_pool) {
773     lws_context_destroy(server->context);
774     server->context = NULL;
775     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_THREAD, "Failed to create WebSocket handler thread pool");
776   }
777 
778   /* Register WebSocket server with named registry */
779   char ws_port_name[32];
780   snprintf(ws_port_name, sizeof(ws_port_name), "ws:%d", server->port);
781   NAMED_REGISTER(server, ws_port_name, "websocket_server", "0x%tx", NULL);
782 
783   /* Register server's sync primitives with hierarchical naming */
784   NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&server->running, "is_running", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(server));
785 
786   /* Register WebSocket context as child of server */
787   NAMED_REGISTER_WEBSOCKET(server->context, "context", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(server));
788 
789   log_info("WebSocket server initialized on port %d with static file serving", config->port);
790   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
791 }






References atomic_store_bool(),
    lws_log_init_server(), and
  thread_pool_create_with_workers().


Referenced by acds_main(), and server_main().






asciichat_error_t
  websocket_server_run (websocket_server_t * server)


Definition at line 793 of file
    lib/network/websocket/server.c.


793                                                                    {
794   if (!server || !server->context) {
795     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid server");
796   }
797 
798   log_info("WebSocket server starting event loop on port %d", server->port);
799 
800   // Run libwebsockets event loop
801   uint64_t last_service_ns = 0;
802   int service_call_count = 0;
803   while (atomic_load_bool(&server->running)) {
804     // Service libwebsockets with 16ms timeout to match 60 FPS frame rate.
805     // This provides frequent event processing (~60 callback invocations per second) so that
806     // WebSocket frames queued by the render loop are sent promptly, matching TCP performance.
807     // All client connections share this single server context, so a single lws_service() call
808     // services fragments and events for all clients simultaneously.
809     uint64_t service_start_ns = time_get_ns();
810     if (last_service_ns && service_start_ns - last_service_ns > 30 * US_PER_MS_INT) {
811       // > 30ms gap between service calls
812       double gap_ms = (double)(service_start_ns - last_service_ns) / 1e6;
813       log_info_every(1 * US_PER_MS_INT, "[LWS_SERVICE_GAP] %.1fms gap between lws_service calls", gap_ms);
814     }
815     service_call_count++;
816     log_debug_every(500 * US_PER_MS_INT, "[LWS_SERVICE] Call #%d, context=%s", service_call_count,
817                     NAMED_DESCRIBE(server->context, "websocket_server"));
818     int result = lws_service(server->context, 1);
819     if (result < 0) {
820       log_error("libwebsockets service error: %d", result);
821       break;
822     }
823   }
824 
825   log_info(
826       "WebSocket server event loop exited (context will be destroyed by main thread after handler threads complete)");
827   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
828 }






References atomic_load_bool(), and
  time_get_ns().
